lines.text: Hmm... The door seems a little strange...
The rocks must’ve smashed the supporting beam, so the wall tilted inside and jammed the door.
It would seem repairs are unnecessary. Better to tear everything down and rebuild.
Well, things breaking down isn’t necessarily a bad thing. No construction without destruction. Don’t you agree?
This is the place, let's go in.
Astonishing, {PlayerName}! Aren’t you just full of surprises?
Well... Let's see here. This Induction Cell seems to be intact. Once we install this back to where it belongs, all it takes for me is to adjust some settings to restore the station’s data.
You work fast, {PlayerName}. You’ll make for a great engineer. Have you considered becoming one?
Ahh. That’s a shame. But I agree that one should decide their own path and walk it on their own free will.
Haha. You were quick to answer. Why don’t you give it some more experience before you come to a final decision?
It is done! Data link complete... We now have access to the Tacet Discord incursion on record.
Restoring the data is only a temporary solution. With this record, we can study the TDs’ behaviors, in order to better formulate measures to strengthen our defenses and eliminate future problems.
Which is also the ultimate goal of my assignment... Performing constant repairs is simply unfeasible long-term.
As you can see, all three surveillance stations are currently damaged. It will take a significant amount of time for even the most skilled engineers to repair and have them fully back and running.
Not to mention the numerous dangers they may encounter along the way...
Ahem. In short, our work at this station has concluded. We should move on to the next one.
I shall entrust you for protection if we come across anything unexpected.
There's not a door to block us this time, but the Cell is missing.
Err... This isn’t really your fault, is it?
Constant moaning about these matters won't do you any good... You are familiar with this place more than any of us. Why don't you try recalling where the Cell may be?
Hmm... It's a clue at least. Lead the way. We should talk with the Exiles nearby.
It's a rectangular piece with smooth edges. It has a metallic frame, cold on the hands, and is rather heavy to carry...
It appears we are short on screening options for the time being. We’ll just have to resort to the old-fashioned way.
One moment. There’s one more thing I’d like to ask.
Do you recall seeing anyone else besides the Rangers recently?
I see...
Well, it’s more than that...
It’s a long story. Let’s focus on the task at hand first.
Hmmm... There. It’s connected!
I’ve managed to gain acces of data on the TDs’ behaviors as well... So the reports about how quickly they’re adapting were true...
How is this possible...?
One more surveillance station to go and the radars can be back online.
It seems the only way we can solve this mystery is by getting the last set of data...
Before we do, there’s more work ahead of us.
Hmm... Now that the last Cell is back in place, the entire line has been connected, so it’ll take some time for the data to transfer.
What the...
I admire your confidence, but be cautious not to get too overconfident.
These TDs may look large in size, but as long as we can figure out their frequencies and exploit their weaknesses, we may be able to gain the upper hand.
We should take the initiative and attack from our side to widen our chances of winning.
Whew— Outstanding work!
One moment. The frequencies of these TDs seem very unusual. I’d like to collect some data.
It is done. We should head back to the surveillance station for a further analysis.
Data link complete... TD behavior record download complete...
Just like my mentor said, the TDs in this area have evolved. They now coordinate their attacks and finish off their targets...
Yes. My mentor. The engineer that was last assigned to this station.
A while ago, I received a message from him mentioning the TDs in this area were evolving in abnormal speed. His speculations are now proven true after witnessing what had happened today for myself.
I've collected most of the data mentioned in the message, however...
The reason for this evolution remains a mystery. The same can be said about the whereabouts of my mentor...
Yes... I haven't heard from him since he sent me the message...
Considering the speed at which the TDs are evolving, I'm afraid he...
Another empty building...
There's one last approach—activate the radar.
Once done, we may find new information from the station’s data.
I can’t say for certain there will be any. But at this point, there’s only one way to find out...
The last data... Our last hope.
Hello there. Are you the ones who’s taken the cell?
Err... Not caramel. Cell.
It seems luck is on our side. We have the Cell!
You may be surprised at what you can find even in garbage. Perhaps there are items in those other piles that would prove useful to us as well...
*chuckle* I'm fine with either. Let’s leave the decision to {PlayerName}.
What did I say!
In the end, there’s always a balance in between fortune and misfortune.
Once a bad luck passes, a good luck would follow.
Why don’t we take advantage of our good luck and search the other piles? We might find even more treasures...
Haha. Let’s leave the decision to {PlayerName}.!
What did I say!
In the end, there’s always a balance in between fortune and misfortune.
Once a bad luck passes, a good luck would follow.
Why don’t we take advantage of our good luck and search the other piles? We might find even more treasures...
Haha. Let’s leave the decision to {PlayerName}.!
What did I say!
In the end, there’s always a balance in between fortune and misfortune.
Once a bad luck passes, a good luck would follow.
Think of it this way. We gained some useful items searching the first two garbage piles, and now we have the Cell as well.
I’d say it’s a fruitful outcome! A form of good fortune, wouldn’t you say so?
Frequencies. Mixed with chaos and clamor...
Dozens of Elite Class TDs... and ten individuals who fought to their last.
Among them were nine elites and one untrained.
The nine people fought against the TDs while protecting the untrained.
The frequency gradually weakened and disappeared... Only one TD and the untrained person remained.
Later the frequency demonstrated a steady pattern, and the waveform resembled the comparative experiments often carried out in laboratories...
But this time, it was done on the field.
The slight tremors on the waveform reveal the agony of being torn apart...
Yes.
After completing a set of comparative experiments, the person ended their own life.
After completing a set of comparative experiments, the person ended their own life.
The TD followed suit by performing the same action...
...I've managed to calm down a bit. I feel much better now.
To be honest. From the moment I received my mentor’s message, I had a feeling that his fate has been sealed.
Being a strict person that he always is, he never talks about anything other than research.
But in that message, besides the research data, there were also a few words of advice.
"This work proved to be a very dangerous one. Should you continue, you risk losing your loved ones, friends... and everything you hold dear till this day."
"Looking back from the end, the data you worked so hard to obtain is but a candle in the wind, always on the verge of being extinguished."
"If you still decide to carry on despite knowing all of this."
"Then come to the Desorock Highland. It's time for you to take over."
Well, you’ve done quite well yourself all this time.
You were with us the whole time, providing information and clearing paths... You even brought along a  strong helper like {PlayerName}.
You and your squad have fulfilled your duties well.
On the battlefield, your sacrifices and resistance is what prevented the radar from suffering any major damage.
And later your tireless efforts paved way for the swift repairs of the radar.
All of this wouldn’t have been achieved without any of us.
We are in your debt, {PlayerName}. You had no obligation to do so, yet your aid is inmeasurable.
I shall double me efforts in studying these precious data we have worked so hard to collect.
I shall continue to study these precious data we managed to collect.

After analysing the waveforms, I have come to a preliminarily conclusion that the abnormal evolution rate of the Tacet Discords in this area is correlated to the surrounding environment and the simulation subjects.
The Rangers who fought till the end.
TDs imitate behaviors to evolve. The more they fight, the faster they evolve.
TDs can only "imitate" and cannot truly learn. They are merely a jumble of chaotic frequencies, not intelligent beings.
They bear no distinguish between good or evil, right from wrong. They simply imitate, prey, and attack...
As long as we make use of this quality, we can bend the situation to our advantage and develop defensive technologies to prevent them from causing harm the surveillance and radar stations ever again.
The data that my mentor left behind for me at the end was a clear indication in this direction... I will devote all my efforts into promoting this technology.
What do we have here...? The radar's detection accuracy has gone up and revealed a few uncharted areas after it was brought back online.
My mentor once told me that these kinds of places usually store unknown treasures. Obtaining this precious data today is more than adequate for me, so I'll leave these treasures to you, {PlayerName}.
I hope you can also experience the surprise and excitement of exploring the unknown.
